title: JSON Schema Governance — Centralized Standards, Validation, and Type Generation
context: The Tribunus project uses JSON Schema for documentation and validation across multiple subsystems (review manifests, verification records, debug packets, runtime events, desktop themes, tool call summaries, and coordination tasks). As the number of schemas grows past 15, maintaining consistency, avoiding duplication, and ensuring long-term maintainability becomes challenging. The current schemas vary in structure, metadata completeness, and filesystem location, making them harder to maintain and validate. Technical context: JSON Schema 2020-12 is now the de facto standard — ratified as IETF Proposed Standard (draft-ietf-jsonschema-json-schema-00, May 2026) — with all major tools (Ajv v8.20.0, Spectral v6.13.0, sourcemeta) fully migrated from draft-07. The $id system now supports absolute URIs with $anchor/$dynamicAnchor for extensible schemas, unevaluatedProperties extends validation across composition boundaries (allOf/anyOf/oneOf), and $defs replaces the deprecated definitions keyword. The schema registry (schemas/index.json) has known defects: duplicate builder_publication_record entry, missing commas, stray braces, and $id domain inconsistency (opencode.ai vs the mandated tribunus.dev). Type generation from schemas via json-schema-to-typescript v15.0.4 is functional but has known limitations — oneOf exclusivity (TS union is inclusive OR), unevaluatedProperties, patternProperties (index signature loses regex), and if/then/else conditional types have no TypeScript equivalents. OpenAPI 3.1 fully adopts 2020-12, meaning Tribunus schemas are reusable in future OpenAPI-described APIs without translation. Options considered and rejected: JSON Type Definition (RFC 8927, too restrictive, no unevaluatedProperties or composition), Zod code-first (schema-first governance principle), TypeBox (added dependency, no toolchain alignment), manual types (defeats schema-first goal).
decision: Adopt a centralized schema governance model grounded in JSON Schema 2020-12 (IETF draft-ietf-jsonschema-json-schema-00). All schemas MUST use JSON Schema 2020-12 ($schema: https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema). Properties are camelCase. Definitions use PascalCase. Filenames are kebab-case with .schema.json extension prefixed by subsystem (e.g., rig.relay.review_manifest.v1.schema.json). All schemas MUST include four metadata fields: $schema, $id (absolute URI on tribunus.dev domain), title, and description. SHOULD include examples and default values where applicable. Deprecated schemas MUST set deprecated: true in the registry entry. Reusable definitions are extracted to schemas/defs.json under the $defs keyword. The schemas/index.json registry is the single source of truth for all schema entries — every schema MUST be registered. Fix known defects in schemas/index.json: deduplicate builder_publication_record, add missing comma before roadmap entry, remove stray closing brace before the schemas array, reconcile $id domain from opencode.ai to tribunus.dev across all schemas and the index. Validation pipeline: (1) pre-commit hook runs ajv meta-schema validation and Spectral linting against a custom ruleset enforcing ADR conventions (naming, metadata, domain, $schema version); (2) CI on push runs ajv-cli validation against all schemas plus Spectral full ruleset; (3) CI on merge generates TypeScript types via json-schema-to-typescript v15.0.4 programmatic API (not bare npx) and runs tsc --noEmit to catch type errors. Type generation uses json-schema-to-typescript v15.0.4 programmatic API for error handling and config control. Tooling versions: Ajv v8.20.0 with strict mode (strictSchema, strictNumbers, strictRequired, strictTypes, strictTuples all enabled), ajv-formats for standard format validation, @stoplight/spectral-cli v6.13.0 with custom ruleset. Validate boolean schemas, const, if/then/else, unevaluatedProperties, prefixItems, and $anchor/$dynamicAnchor where applicable. Documented type-generation limitations: oneOf/anyOf map to TS union (|) but oneOf exclusivity is lost; unevaluatedProperties have no TS equivalent; patternProperties degrade to index signatures; if/then/else requires manual narrowing. For complex polymorphic schemas requiring robust oneOf/anyOf type generation, evaluate quicktype v23.2.6 as an alternative. All schemas must be resolvable via absolute $id URIs (https://tribunus.dev/schemas/...) for future publication. Versioning strategy: $id-embedded version (e.g., https://tribunus.dev/schemas/roadmap/v1/roadmap.json) with SemVer semantics — major=breaking, minor=compatible additions, patch=fixes. CI gate rejects non-compliant schemas.
consequences: Positive: consistent 2020-12 dialect across all schemas, single $defs source reduces duplication, automated validation (Ajv strict mode + Spectral) catches structural errors early, generated TypeScript types improve IDE support and catch runtime/data mismatches, alignment with OpenAPI 3.1 enables future reuse of schemas in API specs without translation, absolute $id URIs support eventual schema publication. Negative: migration effort for existing schemas — $id domain change from opencode.ai to tribunus.dev, directory structure may need reorganization, breaking changes to old $ref URIs. Known type-generation gaps require manual annotation for: oneOf exclusivity (all values may match, not just one), unevaluatedProperties (enforced at runtime only), patternProperties (index signature with string key), if/then/else conditionals (require manual narrowing), $dynamicRef (poor tool support — manual types needed for polymorphic schemas). Mitigation: incremental migration starting with most critical schemas (review_manifest, verification_record, debug_packet), backward compatibility via parallel $id aliases during transition, automated tooling for updates and generation, documented workarounds for each type-generation limitation. Registry fix items: deduplicate builder_publication_record entry, fix missing comma before roadmap entry, remove stray closing brace, reconcile $id domains.
